description_long: This object is a classic three-tined pitchfork, featuring a long, straight handle and a robust forked head. The tines are evenly spaced and sharply pointed, designed for efficient penetration and lifting of materials such as hay, leaves, or debris. The handle appears to be cylindrical and textured for grip, likely made of metal, providing both strength and durability. The fork head is seamlessly attached to the handle, with each tine tapering to a fine point. The entire object is a uniform dark color, suggesting a protective coating or finish. There are no visible signs of rust, wear, or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian and modern appearance.
description: A dark, three-tined metal pitchfork with a long, textured handle and sharply pointed prongs. The design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for heavy-duty urban or garden use.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all three evenly spaced, pointed tines and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the tines and handle, with minimal width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the three tines and handle in full alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and straight alignment of the tines and handle.
